text.title: Audience Signals, Bidding Progression, and Learning Phase
text.summary: Adding audience signals, bidding strategy progression from Maximize Conversions to Target CPA/ROAS, learning phase rules, and asset refresh cadence.

rule.statement: Use your best-converting customer-match list as the primary audience signal; generic interest segments are much less effective at guiding PMax's optimizer.
rule.rationale: PMax weights converter-similarity heavily. A customer-match list of actual buyers gives the optimizer strong signal. Interest segments give broad demographic hints that PMax often ignores.

list.item: Audience signals are hints, not constraints — PMax can and will expand beyond them
Include: converting customer-match list, remarketing lists, similar audiences
Start broad, refine based on asset group performance labels after 4-6 weeks

antipattern.description: Using audience signals as the only targeting control and expecting PMax to respect them as hard limits.
antipattern.reason: Audience signals guide the optimizer but do not restrict inventory. PMax will bid outside the signal audiences if the optimizer finds higher conversion probability elsewhere.

rule.statement: Run Maximize Conversions without a target for at least 14 days and 30+ conversions; then switch to Target CPA or Target ROAS.
rule.rationale: Setting a target CPA/ROAS before sufficient data forces the bidder to over-fit on sparse conversions. The learning phase needs unconstrained data collection to train the model.

rule.statement: Keep daily budget at minimum 3x target CPA; below this, PMax stalls on auction-bidding constraints and cannot exit learning.
rule.rationale: PMax needs budget headroom to explore inventory and conversion paths. A budget too close to the target CPA eliminates any exploration margin and traps the campaign in learning indefinitely.

list.item: Days 1-14: Maximize Conversions, no target — unconstrained exploration
After 30+ conversions: add Target CPA or Target ROAS
Weeks 1-6: minimize pauses and restarts — each restart resets the 4-6 week learning clock

rule.statement: Replace LOW-labeled assets at least quarterly; do not trigger creative swap on performance labels under 7 days old since labels lag 7-14 days.
rule.rationale: Asset performance labels have a 7-14 day lag. Swapping assets on day 3 based on PENDING or early LOW labels churns creative unnecessarily and disrupts the optimizer's learning.

rule.statement: Add account-level negative keywords for brand terms to prevent PMax from cannibalizing branded Search campaigns.
rule.rationale: PMax and branded Search campaigns compete in the same auction. Without brand exclusions, PMax steals credit for high-intent branded queries at higher cost than a dedicated Search campaign.
